title: ValidateCampaignSpec
description:
Input spec for the ``validate-campaign`` workflow primitive.

Most fields are optional — every atomic validator is independently
skippable. A workflow with all atoms disabled returns a passing
report with no findings (still useful as a smoke test of the
composer wiring).

executor_module.description: Dotted Python import path of the user's executor module (e.g. 'src.train'). When set, validate-executor-signatures runs against ``tasks.py.resolve(0)``'s kwargs.
executor_module.title: Executor Module

executor_function.description: Function name in ``executor_module`` whose signature is checked.
executor_function.title: Executor Function

dataset_path.description: Path to the input dataset (parquet / csv / jsonl).
dataset_path.title: Dataset Path

dataset_row_indices.description: Indices that ``tasks.py`` references; validated against actual length + non-null cols.
dataset_row_indices.title: Dataset Row Indices

workload_tags.description: Project-specific tags (e.g. 'attn-fp32', 'mixed-precision') looked up against ``.hpc/playbook.yaml`` known-bad combos. Empty list disables the playbook lookup.
workload_tags.title: Workload Tags

campaign_id.description: Closed-loop campaign slug. When set together with ``expected_cmd_sha``, the workflow invokes validate-stochastic-marker to detect cmd_sha collisions against prior iterations of this campaign — catches the Optuna / random-search / PBT silent-dedup bug class.
campaign_id.title: Campaign Id

expected_cmd_sha.description: The cmd_sha the about-to-submit run will have. Required alongside ``campaign_id`` to enable the stochastic-marker check; ignored otherwise.
expected_cmd_sha.title: Expected Cmd Sha
